name: interp_of_entry_between_external_body
code:
proof fn phi_1_interp_of_entry_between_external_body(d: Directory, i: nat, va: nat, pte: PTE)
    requires
        d.inv(),
        i < d.entries.len(),
        d.interp_of_entry(i).contains_pair(va, pte),
    ensures
        d.entry_base(i) <= va,
        va < d.next_entry_base(i),
{
    d.lemma_interp_of_entry_between(i, va, pte);
}
reason: `lemma_interp_of_entry_between` is `external_body` — VA and frame size bounds for entry interpretations are trusted without proof

name: interp_of_entry_frame_bound_external_body
code:
proof fn phi_2_interp_of_entry_frame_bound_external_body(d: Directory, i: nat, va: nat, pte: PTE)
    requires
        d.inv(),
        i < d.entries.len(),
        d.interp_of_entry(i).contains_pair(va, pte),
    ensures
        d.entry_base(i) < va + pte.frame.size,
        va + pte.frame.size <= d.next_entry_base(i),
{
    d.lemma_interp_of_entry_between(i, va, pte);
}
reason: The frame size upper bound `va + frame.size <= next_entry_base` is also external_body — if wrong, frames could extend past entry boundaries

name: lemma_entry_base_strict_monotone_external_body
reason: `lemma_entry_base_from_index` is `external_body` — strict monotonicity of entry base addresses is trusted without proof

name: next_entry_leq_next_base_external_body
reason: The gap-free property `next_entry_base(i) <= entry_base(j)` for `i < j` is external_body — if wrong, entry ranges could overlap

name: interp_aux_va_upper_bounded
reason: The verified `lemma_interp_aux_between` bounds all VA keys below `upper_vaddr` — depends on both external_body axioms for correctness
